THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING BUT NOTES THAT IT IS ONLY AWARDING THE AMOUNT OF ATTORNEY'S FEES THAT IS STATED IN DEFENDANTS' FILED MOTION, AND THAT ANY ADDITIONAL FEES REQUESTED BY DEFENDANTS WOULD NEED TO BE SOUGHT BY THE FILING OF AN ADDITIONAL MOTION. HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS CLARA JEFFERY, SAMANTHA MICHAELS, LILLIAN KALISH, THE FOUNDATION FOR NATIONAL PROGRESS D/B/A MOTHER's Motion For Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 425.16.

Matter argued and submitted.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling but notes that it is only awarding the amount of attorney's fees that is stated in defendants' filed motion, and that any additional fees requested by defendants would need to be sought by the filing of an additional motion.

Defendants' unopposed "motion for attorney's fees pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. 425.16" is granted.

Order signed.

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar May-03-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ANDREW M. HUTCHINSON, Esq. of Cozen O'Connor (Tel: 415-644-0914) for plaintiff;
Also present: Atty. JOHN B. AGNETTI, Esq. of Hoffman Larin & Agnetti, P.A. (Tel: 305-653-5555) as proposed counsel pro hac vice for plaintiff.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DESIREE CACHETTE'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed Mr. Agnetti to appear to discuss any recent California pro hac vice applications. Mr. Agnetti indicates he has not had any California pro hac vice applications within the last two years.

The Court rules as follows: Plaintiff's motion to admit John B. Agnetti as counsel pro hac vice is granted.

Plaintiff's counsel to submit a proposed order to the Court for review/signature.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
